page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-192-metal is a compute-optimized bare metal server powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 4.0 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 512 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, which provides 2.67 GB of RAM per core. This server does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and PassMark CPU physics tests all scoring in the top 10%. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for integer and tile operations, which contributes to its top-tier performance in PassMark extended instructions and encryption tests. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling in the average range, and text processing benchmarks placing in the bottom 25%.

Memory performance is highly efficient, utilizing DDR5 technology to achieve top-tier read and write bandwidth benchmarks for both small and large blocks. This high memory throughput supports demanding workloads such as database operations and high-volume Redis SET operations, both of which rank in the top 10% of benchmarked systems. Static web serving throughput also reaches top-tier levels.

Given its bare metal architecture and high-density compute resources, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring direct hardware access without virtualization overhead. It is suited for high-throughput database operations, parallelized software compilation, cryptographic tasks, and static web serving at scale, though it is less optimal for single-threaded text processing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-192-metal is a compute-optimized bare metal server featuring 192 vCPUs and 512 GB of DDR5 RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 4.0 GHz, this x86_64 system delivers top-tier multi-core performance, as evidenced by its top 10% rankings in stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU benchmarks. The hardware includes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, scoring in the top 10% for both read and write operations. This configuration is highly effective for parallelized workloads, database operations, and high-throughput web serving, though single-core text processing represents a performance tradeoff.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-192-metal is a compute-optimized bare metal server with 192 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8481C CPU, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-192-metal is a bare metal server with 192 vCPUs and 512 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for high-compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare metal server featuring 192 vCPUs and DDR5 memory, optimized for parallel computing and high-throughput database workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 4.0 GHz.
512 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth.
Advanced hardware acceleration including Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets.
Top-tier performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks, database operations, and Redis SET operations.
Bare metal deployment model with no hypervisor overhead, local storage, or GPU.
Best suited for high-throughput database operations, heavy parallel processing, and static web serving.
